title: Body Scan & Tension Release
subtitle: Check in. Let go. Come home to your body.

cues.text:
Lie down or sit back comfortably.
Close your eyes.
Take three deep breaths, to arrive.

Let your body be still.
We're going to travel through it slowly —
not to fix anything. Just... to notice.

Start at the top of your head.
Notice your scalp.
Is there tightness there? Just breathe into it. Let it soften.

Move to your forehead.
The space between your eyebrows.
The tiny muscles around your eyes. Let them go.

Your cheeks... your jaw... your mouth.
Let your teeth part slightly.
Let your tongue drop from the roof of your mouth.

Down into your neck.
The sides... the back.
So much tension lives here.
Breathe in... and on the exhale, let it release.

Your shoulders.
Let them fall, away from your ears, down toward the floor.
A little more. There.

Down your arms —
your upper arms... elbows... forearms... wrists.
Your hands. Let your fingers uncurl.

Your chest, let it be soft. Not held.
Your ribs... your sides.
Your belly, let it rise and fall freely.

Your lower back.
Notice anything stored there.
Breathe into it... gently.

Your hips... your glutes... your pelvis.
Let them be heavy and still.

Your thighs... your knees.
Your calves... your shins.
All the way down to your ankles.

Your feet.
Your heels against the floor.
Your toes, let them relax... completely.

Your whole body, from head to toe.
Heavy. Warm. Still.

Take one last full breath in —
and let it go... completely.

When you're ready, begin to wake the body gently.
Wiggle your fingers and toes.
A slow roll of the neck.
And open your eyes.
